definitions.text: The pointing of a weapon, as a gun, a dart, or an arrow, or object, in the line of direction with the object intended to be struck; the line of fire; the direction of anything, such as a spear, a blow, a discourse, a remark, towards a particular point or object, with a view to strike or affect it.
examples: Take time with the aim of your gun.

definitions.text: The point intended to be hit, or object intended to be attained or affected.

examples: My number one aim in life is to make money to make my parents, siblings, and kids happy.

definitions.text: To point or direct a missile, or a weapon which propels as missile, towards an object or spot with the intent of hitting it
examples: He aimed at the target, but the arrow flew straight over it.

definitions.text: To direct the intention or purpose; to attempt the accomplishment of a purpose; to try to gain; to endeavor;—followed by at, or by an infinitive
examples: to aim to do well in life

definitions.text: To direct or point (e.g. a weapon), at a particular object; to direct, as a missile, an act, or a proceeding, at, to, or against an object
examples: to aim an arrow at the deer
She aimed a punch at her ex-boyfriend.

definitions.text: Initialism of America Online AIM; AOL Instant Messenger.
